description: country in Eastern Europe and Northern Asia
description_wiki: Russia, or the Russian Federation, is a country in Eastern Europe and North Asia.
Canada is a country in North America.

description: capital city of the United States of America
description_wiki: Washington, D.C., officially the District of Columbia and commonly known as simply Washington or D.C., is the capital city and federal district of the United States.

description: Abrahamic monotheistic ethnic religion of the Jews
description_wiki: Judaism (Hebrew: יַהֲדוּת, romanized: Yahăḏūṯ) is an Abrahamic, monotheistic, ethnic religion that comprises the collective spiritual, cultural, and legal traditions of the Jewish people.

description: a movement to create and develop a Jewish state in the Levant
description_wiki: Zionism is an ethnocultural nationalist movement that emerged in late 19th-century Europe to establish and support a Jewish homeland through colonization in the region of Palestine, which roughly corresponds to the Land of Israel in Judaism—itself central to Jewish history.

aliases: Young Men’s and Young Women’s Hebrew Association movement
description_wiki: A Jewish Community Center or Centre (JCC) is a general recreational, social, and fraternal organization serving the Jewish community in a number of cities.
Israel, officially the State of Israel, is a country in the Southern Levant region of West Asia.

surface_forms: the forest and monument of the John F. Kennedy Peace Forest

description: United States' official memorial to the Holocaust in Washington, D.C.
description_wiki: The United States Holocaust Memorial Museum (USHMM) is the United States' official memorial of the Holocaust, dedicated to the documentation, study, and interpretation of the Holocaust.

entity: Jewish Community Council of Greater Washington
surface_forms: Jewish Community Council of Greater Washington
wikipedia_title: Jewish Community Council of Greater Washington
wikipedia_url: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jewish Community Council of Greater Washington

aliases: National Jewish Community Relations Advisory Council

description: original song written and composed by Rodgers and Hart; from the 1940 musical "Pal Joey"
description_wiki: "I Could Write a Book" is a show tune from the 1940 Rodgers and Hart musical Pal Joey, where it was introduced by Gene Kelly and Leila Ernst.
